text.title: Docker Compose Patterns for Solo VPS Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Docker Compose Patterns for Solo VPS` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Stateful services in compose MUST bind ports to `127.0.0.1:` not `0.0.0.0:`. Wildcard binds bypass UFW = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Docker publishes ports by binding to 0.0.0.0 which inserts iptables rules ABOVE UFW; the port is publicly reachable despite UFW deny-by-default.

rule.statement: Every container MUST declare a `healthcheck` block (interval, timeout, retries). Missing healthcheck = REJECT for stateful services.
rule.rationale: Without healthcheck, `docker compose ps` shows 'running' even when Postgres is mid-init; depends_on cannot gate.

rule.statement: depends_on MUST use `condition: service_healthy` for stateful dependencies. Plain depends_on = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Plain `depends_on` only waits for container start, not readiness; app crashes connecting to mid-init Postgres.

rule.statement: Persistent data MUST use named volumes, not bind mounts or anonymous volumes. Anonymous = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Anonymous volumes accumulate untracked after recreate; named volumes are inspectable and backuppable.

rule.statement: restart policy MUST be `unless-stopped` for stateful services. `no` or `always` = REJECT.
rule.rationale: `always` restarts after manual `docker stop` (annoying); `no` doesn't restart after host reboot. `unless-stopped` is the middle ground.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
